en: The city is holy to the three monotheistic religions - Judaism, Christianity and Islam, and serves as a spiritual, religious, and cultural center.
gn: Kóva hína táva marãngatu mbohapy jeroviapy hemimo’ã peteĩvape g̃uarã: judaísmo, cristianismo ha Islam, ha ojeiporu centro espiritual, religioso ha cultural-ramo.

en: Due to the religious significance of the city, and in particular the many sites of the Old City area, Jerusalem is one of the main tourist destinations in Israel.
gn: Ko táva oguereko rupi peteĩ importancia religiosa, ha’ehaichaite, heta hendápe Táva Tuja ryepýpe, Jerusalén oĩ destino turístico principal-ramo Israel-pe.

en: Jerusalem has many historic, archeological and cultural sites, along with vibrant and crowded shopping centers, cafés, and restaurants.
gn: Jerusalén oguereko heta sitio histórico, arqueológico ha cultural, centro comercial, café ha restaurante ijegua ha ojehoiteha.

en: Ecuador requires that Cuban citizens receive an invitation letter prior to entering Ecuador through international airports or frontier admission points.
gn: Ecuador oikotevẽ umi Cuba-guápe toguahẽ raẽ peteĩ carta ñepyrũrã oike hag̃ua Ecuador-pe aeropuerto internacional térã punto de admisión fronterizo rupive.

en: This letter must be legalized by the Ecuadorian Ministry of Foreign Affairs, and comply with certain requirements.
gn: Ministerio de Relaciones Exteriores de Ecuador omboje va’erã pe carta ha ojeguerekopa va’erã ambue oñeikotevẽva.

en: These requirements are designed to provide an organized migratory flow between both countries.
gn: Ko’ã oñeikotevẽva oñembosako’i ombohape hag̃ua peteĩ flujo migratorio mokõive tetã omohendáva.

en: Cuban citizens who are US green card holders should visit an Ecuadorian Consulate to obtain an exemption to this requirement.
gn: Umi Cuba tavaygua orekóva hérape tarjeta verde ome’ẽva EE.UU. oho va’erã Ecuador consulado-pe ani hag̃ua ojejerure chupekuéra umi oñeikotevẽva.

en: Your passport must be valid for at least 6 months beyond your travel dates. A round/onward trip ticket is needed to prove the length of your stay.
gn: Pasaporte orekóva oiko va’erã sa’ivérõ 6 jasýrupi viaje ára guive. Oñeikotevẽ boletoida y vuelta ohechauka hag̃ua mboy árapa opytáta.

en: Tours are cheaper for larger groups, so if you're by yourself or with just one friend, try to meet other people and form a group of four to six for a better per-person rate.
gn: Ñeikundaha hepy’ive atyguasúpe g̃uarã, upévare reimérõ ne añonte térã peteĩ ne angirũ ndive, eñeha’ã eñomoirũ ambue tapicha ndive peñembyatývo po térã poteĩ ikatu hag̃uaicha peteĩ teĩ pehepyme’ẽ’ive.

en: However, this shouldn't really be off your concern, because often tourists are shuffled around to fill the cars.
gn: Upéicharamo jepe, natekotevẽi kuri reikuaa upéva, akói umi turista oku’e rupi omyanyhẽ hag̃ua peteĩ mba’yrumýi.

en: It seems actually to be more a way of tricking people into believing they have to pay more.
gn: Añetehápe, vaicha kóva peteĩ hendáicha oñembotavy hag̃ua tapichakuéra toimo’ã ohepyme’ẽ hetave va’erãha.

en: Towering above the north end of Machu Picchu is this steep mountain, often the backdrop to many photos of the ruins.
gn: Ojejupivévo Machu Picchu extremo norte rehe ojejuhu komontaña empinada, heta jey ojekuaa ko ruina ra’ãnga kupépe.

en: It looks a bit daunting from below, and it is a steep and difficult ascent, but most reasonably fit persons should be able to make it in about 45 minutes.
gn: Vaicha ñanemongyhyje nungáva yvýgui ñama’ẽvo hese, hese hasyeterei ojejupi hag̃ua, katu opavave nunga tapichakuéra ikatu va’erã ojupipa 45 aravo’ípe.

en: Stone steps are laid along most of the path, and in the steeper sections steel cables provide a supporting handrail.
gn: Oñemoĩ escalón itaguigua tape tuichavéva pukukue, ha umi hasyveha ojejupi hag̃uame oñemoĩ cable de acero oikóva ñandepojái hag̃ua.

en: That said, expect to be out of breath, and take care in the steeper portions, especially when wet, as it can become dangerous quickly.
gn: Kóicha oje’évo, eha’arõntema ndepytupa hag̃ua ha eñangarekoporã  umi hasyvehápe ojejupi hag̃ua ha ko’ýte he’õ jave, ikaturupi pya’e oĩ peligro.

en: There's a tiny cave near the top that must be passed through, it is quite low and a rather tight squeeze.
gn: Oĩ peteĩ itakua michimi yvate ojahasa va’erãhárupi, ikarape ha nda’ipeiete.

en: Seeing the sites and wildlife of the Galapagos is best done by boat, just as Charles Darwin did it in 1835.
gn: Iporãiteve ojehecha tenda ha teko ka’aguy  Galápagos-pegua peteĩ bote guive, Charles Darwin ojapohaguéicha 1835-pe.

en: Over 60 cruise ships ply the Galapagos waters - ranging in size from 8 to 100 passengers.
gn: 60 cucero arimi ohasa Galápagoa ýrupi, ãva tuicha 8 térã 100 tapicha ija hag̃uáicha.

en: Most people book their place well in advance (as the boats are usually full during the high season).
gn: Opavave nunga tapichakuéra ojeruréma tenonde porã hendag̃ua rehe (umi barco hendyhẽ rupi temporada alta aja).

en: Be sure the agent through whom you book is a Galapagos specialist with a good knowledge of a wide variety of ships.
gn: Ehechákena pe agente neipytyvõva ejerure hag̃ua ne rendag̃ua rehepa hína oikuaa pypuku opa mba’e Galápagos ha opaichagua barco rehegua.

en: This will ensure that your particular interests and/or constraints are matched with the ship most suitable to them.
gn: Upévarupive ikatúta ehecharamo térã ikatu’ỹva rehupyty toho upe barco eikotevẽ reho hag̃ua rehe.

en: Before the Spanish arrived in the 16th century, northern Chile was under Inca rule while the indigenous Araucanians (Mapuche) inhabited central and southern Chile.
gn: España-gua oguahẽmboyve siglo XVI-pe, norte de Chile oĩ va’ekue Inca poguýpe, umi indígena araucano (mapuche) oiko va’ekue centro ha surgotyo Chile-pe.

en: The Mapuche were also one of the last independent American indigenous groups, that were not fully absorbed into Spanish-speaking rule until after Chile's independence.
gn: Umi mapuche avei hína indígena aty America-gua ijeheguiguáva ipahápe opytáva,  ojaho’i’ỹre chupe dominio de habla hispana Chile isãsorire peve.

en: Although Chile declared independence in 1810 (amid the Napoleonic wars that left Spain without a functioning central government for a couple of years), decisive victory over the Spanish was not achieved until 1818.
gn: Jepéramo Chile omboaje isãso 1810 arýpe (guerra napoleónica oheja va’ekue España-pe gobierno central oku’eỹva rehe un para de años aja), victoria decisiva España-gua rovake oñemoañete 1818 arýpe ae.

en: The Dominican Republic (Spanish: República Dominicana) is a Caribbean country that occupies the eastern half of the island of Hispaniola, which it shares with Haiti
gn: Pe República Dominicana ha'e  peteĩ tetã Caribe-gua opytáva kuarahy reikévo mbyte guive yvy'aty Española-pe, ha péva o-comparti Haití ndive.

en: Besides white sand beaches and mountain landscapes, the country is home to the oldest European city in the Americas, now part of Santo Domingo.
gn: Ha umi playa yvy morotĩ ha yvyty yvate paisaje-kuéra rire, ko tetã ombohóga táva Europeo itujavéva América-pe, ko'ág̃a Santo Domingo rehegua.

en: The island was first inhabited by the Taínos and Caribes. The Caribes were an Arawakan-speaking people who had arrived around 10,000 BCE.
gn: Ko isla pe oiko vaekue ñepyrũmby umi Tainos ha Caribes kuéra. Umi Caribe kuéra ha'e vaekue avakuéra oñe'ẽva Arawaka oguahe vaekue aipo 10.000 ary AC.

en: Within a few short years following the arrival of European explorers, the population of Tainos had significantly been reduced by the Spanish conquerors
gn: Oguahẽ riremi umi explorador Europeo, umi conquistador español tuicháma ombohesa’ive kuri Taíno-kuéra reko.

en: Based on Fray Bartolomé de las Casas (Tratado de las Indias) between 1492 and 1498 the Spanish conquerors killed around 100,000 Taínos.
gn: Fray Bartolomé de las Casa taiha rupive (Tratado de las Indias) umi 1492 ha 1498 pe, conquistador Español kuéra ojuka 100.000 Tainos kuéra.

en: Jardín de la Unión. This space was built as the atrium for a 17th-century convent, of which the Templo de San Diego is the sole surviving building.
gn: Jardín de la Unión. Ko tenda oñemopu’ã va’ekue peteĩ convento siglo XVII-pegua atrio-ramo, ichugui hemby ko’agãite Templo de San Diego añoite.

en: It now functions as the central plaza, and always has a lot of things going on, day and night.
gn: Ko’ágã oiko plaza central-ramo ha heta mba’e oiko upépe, arakue ha pyhare.

en: There are a number of restaurants surrounding the garden, and in the afternoons and evening there free concerts are often given from the central gazebo.
gn: Oĩ heta restaurantepe jardín jere rehe ha ka’aru ha pyharekue sapy’ánte oiko concierto ojehepyme’ẽ’ỹva mirador central guive.

en: Callejon del Beso (Alley of the Kiss). Two balconies separated by only 69 centimeters is home of an old love legend.
gn: Callejóndelbeso (Alleyof the kiss). Mokõi balcón ojeívaojuehegui 59 centímetro-nte hína oñembohekoha mborayhu rembiasakue yma.

en: For a few pennies some children will tell you the story.
gn: Centavo mimi rehe oĩ mitã omombe’útava ndéve upe tembiasakue.

en: Bowen Island is a popular day trip or weekend excursion offering kayaking, hiking, shops, restaurants, and more.
gn: Bowen Island hína peteĩ viaje ipukúva peteĩ ára térã peteĩ excursión defin de semana oikuave’ẽva kayak, senderismo, ñemuha, restaurante hahetave mba’e.

en: This authentic community is located in Howe Sound just off Vancouver, and is easily accessed via scheduled water taxis departing Granville Island in downtown Vancouver.
gn: Ko tekoha añetete oĩ Howe Sound-pe, Vancouver yketére, ha ikatu oñeguahẽ upépe taxi acuático oñembosako’íva rupive, osẽva Granville Island Vancouver centro guive.

en: For those who enjoy outdoor activities, a trek up the Sea to Sky corridor is essential.
gn: Umi oguerohoryva okaháre ojehasávape g̃uarã, corredor Sea to Sky rupi jeguata tekotevẽta katuete.

en: Whistler (1.5 hour drive from Vancouver) is expensive but well-known because of the 2010 Winter Olympics.
gn: Whistler (opytáva 1,5 aravo mba’yrumýime Vancouver guive) hepy katu ojeikuaaitemi Juegos Olímpicos de Invierno de 2010 rupive.

en: In the winter, enjoy some of the best skiing in North America, and in the summer try some authentic mountain biking.
gn: Araro’ýpe, ehasaporã umi tenda ojehecharamovéva esquí porã America del Norte-pe, arahakúpe eñeha’ãmi ejapociclísmo de montaña añetete.

en: Permits must be reserved in advance. You must have a permit to stay overnight at Sirena.
gn: Upépe jeikerã ojejerure va’erã voiporã. Oñemoneĩ va’erã ndéveepyta hag̃ua eke Sirena-pe.

en: Sirena is the only ranger station that offers dormitory lodging and hot meals in addition to camping. La Leona, San Pedrillo, and Los Patos offer only camping with no food service.
gn: Sirena añoite hína guardabosques estación oikuave’ẽva ojepyta hag̃ua ojeke ha tembi’u aku ha ikatuhápe oñe-acampa. La Leona, San Pedrillo ha Los Patos oikuave’ẽ camping añónte ha ndorekói tembi’u.

en: It is possible to secure park permits directly from the Ranger Station in Puerto Jiménez, but they do not accept credit cards
gn: Ikatu hína oñemoneĩ ndéve estacionamiento  Tahachi ka'aguy estación guive Puerto Jiménezpe, katu upépe ndojeiporukuaái tarjeta de crédito.

en: The Park Service (MINAE) does not issue park permits more than one month in advance of anticipated arrival.
gn: Servicio de Parques (MINAE) nomoneĩri parque-pe jeikerã ndojejeruréiramo peteĩ jasy tapykueve oñeguahẽseha ára mboyve.

en: CafeNet El Sol offers a reservation service for a fee of US$30, or $10 for one-day passes; details on their Corcovado page.
gn: CafeNet El Sol oikuave’ẽ peteĩ servicio de reservaciones peteĩ tarifa hepýva US $ 30, térã $ 10 peteĩ árape jeikerã rehe; eikuaave Corcovado página guive.

en: The Cook Islands are an island country in free association with New Zealand, located in Polynesia, in the middle of the South Pacific Ocean.
gn: Isla-kuéra Cook hína peteĩ tetã insular ojoajúva sãsóme Nueva Zelanda ndive, opytáva Polinesia-pe, Oceano Pacífico mbytépe.

en: It is an archipelago with 15 islands spread out over 2.2 million km2 of ocean.
gn: Ha'e hína peteĩ archipelago orekóva 15 isla oñemosarãmbíva 2.2 millón km² océano-pe.

en: With the same time zone as Hawaii, the islands are sometimes thought of as "Hawaii down under".
gn: Oreko Hawái zona horaria, umi isla sapy'ánte oje'éjepi hese "Hawái yguypegua"

en: Though smaller, it reminds some elderly visitors of Hawaii before statehood without all the large tourist hotels and other development.
gn: Michĩvéramo jepe, imandu'a ambue Hawái visitante itujávape oĩ mboyve estadía umi hotel turístico tuichávape ha ambue jeguerojera.

en: The Cook Islands do not have any cities but are composed of 15 different islands. The main ones are Rarotonga and Aitutaki.
gn: Isla-kuéra Cook ndoguerekói táva, katu oguereko 15 isla iñambuéva ojoehegui. Umi principal hína Rarotonga ha Aitutaki.

en: In developed countries today, providing deluxe bed and breakfasts has been raised to a sort of art-form.
gn: Tetã ojeguerojeráva ko'agãgua, bed ha breakfast de lujo-va ñeme'ẽ ojehupi peteĩ mab'e jeguakáramo.

en: At the top end, B&Bs obviously compete mainly on two main things: bedding and breakfast.
gn: Apýra yvateguápe, B & B ojeikuaavoi oñoha'ãha añete mokõi mba'e principal-vape, ao tupapegua ha rambosarãme.

en: Accordingly, at the finest such establishments one is apt to find the most luxurious bedding, maybe a handmade quilt or an antique bed.
gn: Upéicha rupi, umi tenda koichagua iporãvévape, peteĩ ijuhukuaa peteĩ ao tupápe guarã, ikatu peteĩ poyvi tapicha pópe ojejapopyréva téra peteĩ tupa yma guare.

en: Breakfast may include seasonal delights of the region or the host's speciality dish.
gn: Rambosarãme ikatu oike mba'e héva ojejuhúva tendápe upe temporada-pe térã plato hevéva ojapóva oga jára.

en: The setting might be an historic old building with antique furnishings, manicured grounds and a swimming pool.
gn: Tenda ikatu peteĩ edificio hembiasa yma guaréva orekóva mueble yma guare, yvotyty oñeñangareko porãva ha peteĩ piscina.

en: Getting into your own car and heading off on a long road trip has an intrinsic appeal in its simplicity.
gn: Ejupíramo ne mba'yrumýime ha esẽ reho mombyry peteĩ tape rehe oguereko peteĩ atractivo ñande ryepýpe guarã simplicidad orekóva rupive.

en: Unlike larger vehicles, you are probably already familiar with driving your car and know its limitations.
gn: Iñambuéva umi mba'yrumýi tuichavévagui, ikatu rejepokuaáma nde mba'yrumýi ñemboguata rehe ha eikuaa porã ijapyrakuéra.

en: Setting up a tent on private property or in a town of any size can easily attract unwanted attention.
gn: Emohenda peteĩ campaña-pegua tienda peteĩ yvy ijáravape térã peteĩ táva oĩmeraichagua tuicháva ikatu ojejesareko hese umi ojeipota'ỹva.

en: In short, using your car is a great way to take a road trip but seldom in itself a way to "camp".
gn: Upéicha, ne mba'yrumýi jeiporu hína oikoite ojejapo ojeho haguã viaje-pe tapére, katu ndojejapói jepi upéva peteĩ camping apóramo.

en: Car camping is possible if you have a large minivan, SUV, Sedan or Station Wagon with seats that lay down.
gn: Mba'yrumýme camping apo ikatu ojeguerekóramo peteĩ minivan tuicháva, SUV, sedán térã station wagon apyka oñenóva rehe.

en: Some hotels have a heritage from the golden age of steam railways and ocean liners; before the Second World War, in the 19th or early 20th centuries.
gn: Ambue hotel oguereko peteĩ herencia opyta va'ekue ferrocarril vapor rehe oikóva ojeiporumeméramo guare ha transatléntico-kuéra; ñorairõ guasu mokõihápe guare, siglo 19 térã siglo 20 oñepyrũramoguare.

en: These hotels were where the rich and the famous of the day would stay, and often had fine dining and nightlife.
gn: Ko'ã hotel hína umi tenda opytahaguépe umi rico ha famoso upe arapegua ha jepivérõ oguereko jekaruha oikoitéva ha pyharépe ñesẽ rehegua.

en: The old fashioned fittings, the lack of the latest amenities, and a certain graceful agedness are also part of their character.
gn: Jeguaka anticuado-kuéra, ndoguerekói comodidad osẽ ramóva ha ambue antiguedad elegante-va oĩ avei carácter oguerekóva apytépe.

en: While they are usually privately owned, they sometimes accommodate visiting heads of state and other dignitaries.
gn: Jepéramo oguereko ijára, sapy'ánte oñemohenda estado mburuvicha ha mbue moakaharakuérape guarã.

en: A traveller with piles of money might consider a round the world flight, broken up with stays in many of these hotels.
gn: Peteĩ viajero orekóva heta viru ikatu oñakãngeta peteĩ jeveve arapy jerére, oñembosa'íva heta ñembohupa ko'ã hotel-pe rehe.

en: A hospitality exchange network is the organization that connects travelers with locals in the cities they are going to visit.
gn: Peteĩ red hospitalidad intercambio apoha hína peteĩ ñembyatypy ombojoapýva viejero-kuéra tendagua ndive umi téva ohohápe.

en: Joining such a network usually just requires filling out an online form; although some networks offer or require additional verification.
gn: Ejoaju haguã peteĩ red ko'ãichaguáre tekotevẽ emyanyhe peteĩ formulario on line; oĩramo jepe ambue red oikotevẽvéva jesareko rehe.

en: A listing of available hosts is then provided either in print and/or online, sometimes with references and reviews by other travelers.
gn: Upe rire oñeme'ẽ peteĩ óga jarakuéra rysýi kuatiápe ha / térã on line, sapy'ánte oguereko referencia ha temiando ome'ẽva ambue viajero.

en: Couchsurfing was founded in January 2004 after computer programmer Casey Fenton found a cheap flight to Iceland but did not have a place to stay.
gn: Couchsurfing oñemopyenda va'ekue jasyteĩ 2004-pe peteĩ programado informático Casey Fenton ojuhu rire peteĩ jeveve hapy sa'íva Islandia-pe ohóva, katu ndoguerekói mamópa opytáta.

en: He emailed students at the local university and received an overwhelming number of offers for free accommodation.
gn: Oguerahauka peteĩ correo electrónico mbo'ehaovusu temimbo'ekuérape ha ojegueruka chupe hetaiterei alojamiento ñeikuave'ẽ.

en: Hostels cater primarily to young people – a typical guest is in their twenties – but you can often find older travellers there too.
gn: Alberguekuéra oñangarekove mitã pyahúre, peteĩ huésped jepiverõguáva oguereko hetave pokõi arýgui, katu py'ỹi avei ejuhukuaa viejero ijary hetavéva.

en: Families with children are a rare sight, but some hostels do allow them in private rooms.
gn: Ogayguakuéra mitãre ndojehechaguasúi, katu oĩ albergue omboejéva koty privado-vape.

en: The city of Beijing in China will be the host city of the Olympic Winter Games in 2022, which will make it the first city to have hosted both the summer and winter Olympics.
gn: Beijing China-gua táva hína oĩta Olimpiada Ñembosarái Araro'ypegua rendáramo 2022-pe, upéva omohendáta chupe táva peteĩha ojejapohaguépe Olimpiada arahaku ha araro'ypegua.

en: Beijing will host the opening and closing ceremonies and the indoor ice events.
gn: Beijing China-gua táva hína oĩta jeguerovy'a guahẽ ha ipahápegua ha umi oikótava hielo-pe techo guýpe.

en: Other skiing events will be at the Taizicheng ski area in Zhangjiakou, about 220 km (140 miles) from Beijing.
gn: Ambue oikótava esquípe oikóta Taizicheng-pe esquí ojejapoha renda Zhangjiakou-pe, opytáva 220 km (140 milla) Beijing-gui.

en: Most of the temples do have an Annual festival starting from November end to Mid of May, which varies according to each temple's annual calendar.
gn: Hetave hín umi tupao orekóva peteĩ ritual anual oñepyrũva jasypateĩ paha guive ha oho jasypo mbyte peve, iñambuéva ojoehegui umi tupaokuérape.

en: Most of the temple festivals is celebrated as part of temple's anniversary or presiding deity's birthday or any other major event associated with the temple.
gn: Hetave umi jeguerovy'a tupaopegua ojejapóva tupao aramboty rehe térã Tupã aramboty omoakãva térã oĩmeraẽ ambue oikótava oñemomba'eguasúva ojoajúva tupao rehe.

en: Kerala's temple festivals are very interesting to see, with regular procession of decorated elephants, temple orchestra and other festivities.
gn: Tupao Kerala-gua jeguerovy'a ojehecharamó jepi, oguereko peteĩ procesión regular oĩhápe elefante oñembojeguapyréva, tupaopegua orquesta ha ambue jeguerovy'a.

en: A World's Fair (commonly called World Exposition, or simply Expo) is large international festival of arts and sciences.
gn: Peteĩ feria mundial (jepivérõ ojeheróva Arapypegua jehechaukaha térã Jehechauka) hína peteĩ jeguerovy'a guasu jeguaka ha kuaapy rehegua.

en: Participating countries present artistic and educational displays in national pavilions to showcase world issues or their country's culture and history.
gn: Tetãnguéra oĩva upépe ohechauka jepi jeguaka ha jehekombo'e rehegua tetãnguéra oñemohendahápe ohechauka haguã arapy ñe'ẽrã ha hetã rembiasakue.

en: International Horticultural Expositions are specialised events which showcase floral displays, botanical gardens and anything else to do with plants.
gn: Tetãnguéra jehechaukaha Horticultura rehegua hína oikótava ojehechaukahápe ñe'ẽrã yvoty rehegua, yvotyty botánico ha oĩmeraẽ mba'e ojoapýva ka'avo rehe.

en: Although in theory they can take place annually (so long as they are in different countries), in practice they are not.
gn: Ojejapokuaáramo jepe ary ha arýpe (oikótaramo peteĩ tetã iñambuévape), ndojejapói jepi upéicha.

en: These events normally last anywhere between three and six months, and are held on sites no smaller than 50 hectares.
gn: Ko'ã oikótava ipuku jepi mbohapy térã poteĩ jasy, ha ojejapo umi tenda oguerekohápe 50 hectárea.

en: There are many different film formats that have been used over the years. Standard 35 mm film (36 by 24 mm negative) is much the commonest.
gn: Oĩ heta ta'ãngamýi formato iñambuéva ojeiporúva ko'ã arýpe. TA'ãngamýi estándar 35 mm (negativo orekóva 36 x 24 mm) hína upe ojeikuaavéva.

en: It can usually be replenished fairly easily if you run out, and gives resolution roughly comparable to a current DSLR.
gn: Jepivéramo, ojemohendakuaa jey jepi hasy'ỹre osẽramo oho, ha ome'ẽ peteĩ resolución oñembohovakuekuaáva DSLR ko'agãguá rehe.

en: Some medium-format film cameras use a 6 by 6 cm format, more precisely a 56 by 56 mm negative.
gn: Ambue cámara ta'ãngamýi-peguava oiporu peteĩ formato 6 x 6 cm, he presisamente peteĩ negativo orekóva 56 x 56 mm rehe.

en: This gives resolution almost four times that of a 35 mm negative (3136 mm2 versus 864).
gn: Kóva ome'ẽ peteĩ resolución tuichavéva 4 tendápe jey ambue negativo 35mm orekóva (3136 mm2 864-pe)

en: Wildlife is among the most challenging motifs for a photographer, and needs a combination of good luck, patience, experience and good equipment.
gn: Mymbakuéra jehecha hekohápe hína upe mba'e desafiante-va peteĩ fotografo-pe guarã, ha oikotevẽ peteĩ ojoapy ojoehe po'a, py'aguapy, hembiasakue ha equipo porã.

en: Wildlife photography is often taken for granted, but like photography in general, a picture is worth a thousand words.
gn: Mymbakuéra hekohápe ñemoha'ãnga hína sapy'ánte oje'e ndahasyiha, katu ambue ta'ãnga ñeguenohẽicha, peteĩ ta'ãnga hepyve hetaiterei ñe'ẽgui.

en: Wildlife photography often requires a long telephoto lens, though things like a flock of birds or a tiny creature need other lenses.
gn: Mymbakuéra hekohápe ñeguenohẽ heta jey oikotevẽ peteĩ teleobjetivo rehe, jepéramo sapy'a peteĩ guyra aty térã peteĩ mymba'i oikotevẽ ambueichagua lente rehe.

en: Many exotic animals are hard to find, and parks sometimes have rules about taking photographs for commercial purposes.
gn: Heta mymba exótico hasy ojejuhu haguã, ha parque-kuéra sapy'ánte oguereko mbojojaha oñeguenohẽ haguã ta'ãnga ojehepyme'ẽ haguã.

en: Wild animals might either be shy or aggressive. The environment might be cold, hot, or otherwise hostile.
gn: Mymba hesaitéva ikatu hi'otĩ ha iñaña. Tekoha ikatu ho'ysã, haku, térã hostil.

en: The world has over 5,000 different languages, including more than twenty with 50 million or more speakers.
gn: Arapy oguereko hetave 5.000 ñe'ẽgui, ijapytépe hetave 20-gui oguerekóva 50 millón térã hetave ñe'ẽhára.

en: Written words are often easier to understand than spoken words, also. This is especially true of addresses, which are often difficult to pronounce intelligibly.
gn: Ñe'ẽnguéra ojehaíva, py'ỹi oñeikumbyve umi oñeñe'ẽvagui, avei. Kóva ojeiporu meme, tenda jeikuaa, hasy jepiva oje'e porã haguã ayvúpe.

en: Many entire nations are completely fluent in English, and in even more you can expect a limited knowledge - especially among younger people.
gn: Heta tetã tuichakue oñe'ẽ inglés-pe, ha jepivérõ ikatu ojekuaa sa'i, hetave jey tapicha imitãvéva apytépe.

en: Imagine, if you will, a Mancunian, Bostonian, Jamaican and Sydneysider sitting around a table having dinner at a restaurant in Toronto.
gn: Eñakãngetamína, ojeipotáramo, peteĩ Manchester-gua, Boston-gua, Jamaica-gua ha Sydney-gua oguapy peteĩ mesa jerére okaru pyhare peteĩ Toronto-gua karuhápe.

en: They're regaling each other with stories from their hometowns, told in their distinct accents and local argot.
gn: Oñomombe'u ojupe tembiasa heñoihague távapegua, omombe'úva acento ha tenda argot oguerekóvape.

en: Buying food in supermarkets is usually the cheapest way to get fed. Without cooking opportunities, choices are however limited to ready-made food.
gn: Hi'upyrã jejogua supermercado-hárehina pe sa'ive hepýva, Tekotevẽ'ỹre ñembojy, poravorã opyta ohóvo tembi'u ojejapopyrévape.

en: Increasingly supermarkets are getting a more varied section of ready-made food. Some even provide a microwave oven or other means to heat food.
gn: Hetave ohóvo, umi supermercado oguereko jepihetave tembi'u variedad ohóvo. Sapy'ánte oĩ omoĩva micoonda horno térã ambue tembiporu oñembyaku haguã hi'upyrã.

en: In some countries or types of stores there is at least one on-site restaurant, often a rather informal one with affordable prices.
gn: Ambue tetãme térã ñemuhakuérape oĩ peteĩ karuha jepe upe tendápe, sapy'ánte informal ha hepy sa'íva.

en: Make and carry copies of your policy and your insurer's contact details with you.
gn: Ejapo ha egueraha nde póliza copia ha ojoaju haguã ne ndive nde aseguradora.

en: They need to show the insurer's e-mail address and international phone numbers for advice/authorizations and making claims.
gn: Ejehechauka va'erã aseguradora correo electrónico ha papapy internacional reguereko haguã ñepytyvõ / ñemoneĩ ha emba'ejerure haguã.

en: Have another copy in your luggage and online (e-mail to yourself with attachment, or stored in the "cloud").
gn: Eguereko peteĩ copia nde equipaje-pe (ejeguerahauka peteĩ correo orekóva archivo adjunto térã ojehupíva araíre)

en: If traveling with a laptop or tablet, store a copy in its memory or disc (accessible without the internet).
gn: Rehóramo peteĩ computadora popegua térã tableta rehe, eñongatu peteĩ copia memoria térã disco-pe (ijeikekuaahápe internet-ỹre).

en: Also give policy/contact copies to traveling companions and relatives or friends back home willing to help.
gn: Ehecha avei eme'ẽ peteĩ póliza copia umi viaje irũ, ogaygua térã ne angirũ reguerekóvape ikatúvape neipipytyvõ.

en: Moose (also known as elk) aren't inherently aggressive, but will defend themselves if they perceive a threat.
gn: Alce, (jeikuaáva alce-kuéraramo) ndaha'éi iñañáva, katu oñepysyrõta oñandúramo amenaza.

en: When people don't see moose as potentially dangerous, they may approach too closely and put themselves at risk.
gn: Tapichakuéra ndohechairamo alce ikatuha peligroso, ikatu oñembojaiterei oñemoĩ peteĩ riesgo-pe.

en: Drink alcoholic drinks with moderation. Alcohol affects everyone differently, and knowing your limit is very important.
gn: Hey'u sa'imi umi bebida alcohólica. Alcohol ojapyhy ambueháicha opavavépe, ha nde apýra jeikuaa iporaiterei.

en: Possible long term health events from excessive drinking can include liver damage and even blindness and death. The potential danger is increased when consuming illegally produced alcohol.
gn: Teãi rehe ojapokuaáva oje'ueterei rupi alcohol hína, ojapo vai hígado ha ikatu oheja ciego ha oñemano. Ha peligro tuichavéva hína uje'úramo umi alcohol ojejapo va'ekue ilegalmente.

en: Illegal spirits can contain various dangerous impurities including methanol, which can cause blindness or death even in small doses.
gn: Licor ilegal.kuéra oguerekokuaa heta mba'e ky'a peligroso-va, oĩkuaahápe metanol, oporohejakaáva ciego térã oporojukakuaa jepéramo oje'u sa'imínte.

en: Eyeglasses can be cheaper in a foreign country, especially in low-income countries where labour costs are lower.
gn: Anteojo-kuéra ikatu hapy sa'ive ambue tetãre, ha ikatuve jey upéicha umi tetã ojeguerekohápe inigreso michĩva tembiapo repy sa'ivehápe.

en: Consider getting an eye exam at home, especially if insurance covers it, and bringing the prescription along to be filed elsewhere.
gn: Ehecha ehechauka nde resa reikohápe, ha especialmente seguro ojaho'íramo, ha egueraha receta ehechauka haguã ambue tendápe.

en: High-end brand-name frames available in such areas may have two problems; some may be knock-offs, and the real imported ones may be more expensive than at home.
gn: Umi marco alta gama-va oĩva umi tendápe ikatu oguereko mokõi apañuái, ambupeva ikatu imitación, térãumi importado añetéva ikatu hepyve reikohágui.

en: Coffee is one of the world's most traded commodities, and you can probably find many types in your home region.
gn: Café hína peteĩ mba'erepy ojehepyme'ẽvéva arapýre, ha katuete ikatu rejuhu hetaichagua región reimehápe.

en: Nevertheless, there are many distinctive ways of drinking coffee around the world that are worth experiencing.
gn: Katu, oĩ heta hendáicha oje'u haguã café mayma tenda rehe, oñetanteakuaáva.

en: Canyoning (or: canyoneering) is about going in a bottom of a canyon, which is either dry or full of water.
gn: Barranquismo (térã: barranquismo) hína ojeho peteĩ cañón ruguápe taha'e orekohápe heta y térã nahániri.

en: Canyoning combines elements from swimming, climbing and jumping--but requires relatively little training or physical shape to get started (compared to rock climbing, scuba diving or alpine skiing, for example).
gn: Barranquismo-pe oñembojoaju elemento jeyta, jejupi ha jepo, katu natekotevẽi heta entrenamiento rehe térã tete ñemongu'e oñemoñepyrũ haguã (ita rehe jejupi rovake, buceo, terã esquí alpino, techapyrãramo)

en: Hiking is an outdoor activity which consists of walking in natural environments, often on hiking trails.
gn: Senderismo hína peteĩ tembiapo okaháre oikóva ijeguatahápe umi tenda natural-va rehe, py'ỹinte oĩ senerismo rape.

en: Day hiking involves distances of less than a mile up to longer distances that can be covered in a single day.
gn: Jeguata arakuépe oike mombyrykue oike mombyrykue sa'ivéva peteĩ milla-guive ohupytýva mombyrykue ikatúva ojejapo peteĩ ára pukukue aja.

en: For a day hike along an easy trail little preparations are needed, and any moderately fit person can enjoy them.
gn: Peteĩ caminata oikóva peteĩ árape guarã peteĩ sendero hasy'ỹva pukukuépe, tekotevẽ ñembosako'i michĩmi ha oĩmeraẽ tapicha hete hu'ũva ohecharamokuaa.

en: Families with small children may need more preparations, but a day outdoors is easily possible even with babies and pre-school children.
gn: Oguayguakuéra orekóva mitã michĩva  ikatu oikotevẽve ñembosako'i, katu peteĩ ára okaháre ndahasyiete jepéramo mitãra'ýre ha mitã oikémava mbo'ehaópe rehe.

en: Internationally, there are nearly 200 running tour organizations. Most of them operate independently.
gn: Tetãnguéra nivel-pe oĩ haimete 200 ñembyatypy gira rehegua oikóva. Heta jey oiko ijeheguínte.

en: The Global Running Tours successor, Go Running Tours networks dozens of sightrunning providers on four continents.
gn: Global Running Tours omongoviáva, Go Runnig Tours, ojoapýva ambue docena sightrunning me'ẽhára rehe irundy continente-pe.

en: With roots in Barcelona's Running Tours Barcelona and Copenhagen's Running Copenhagen, it was quickly joined by Running Tours Prague based in Prague and others.
gn: Hapóva Running Tours Barcelona, Barcelona-gua ha Running Copenhagen, Copenhagen-gua rehe pya'e ojoaju Running Tours Prage orekóva hóga Praga-pe ha ambue.

en: There are many things you have to take into consideration before and when you travel somewhere.
gn: Oĩ heta mba'e reikuaa va'erã eho mboyve peteĩ tendápe.

en: When you travel, expect things not to be like they are "back home". Manners, laws, food, traffic, lodging, standards, language and so on will to some extent differ from where you live.
gn: Ágã esẽvove viaje-pe, ehe'ãrõ va'erã umi mba'e ndaha'emo'ãiha "ogapeguáicha". Modales, leikuéra, tembi'u, tráfico, alojamiento, mbojojaha, ñe'ẽ, ambue mba'e, iñambuéta tenda reikohágui.

en: This is something you always need to keep in mind, to avoid disappointment or perhaps even distaste over local ways to do things.
gn: Kóva peteĩ mba'e reikuaa va'erã, ani rehasa vai térã ani ndepochy reikuaavove mba'éichapa ojejapo ambue mba'e umi tenda rupi.

en: Travel agencies have been around since the 19th century. A travel agent is usually a good option for a journey which extends beyond a traveller's previous experience of nature, culture, language or low-income countries.
gn: Viaje rehegua agencia oĩ ñepyrũ kuri siglo 19 guive. Peteĩ viaje agente ha'e jepi peteĩ opción iporãva peteĩ viaje ojeipysovéva ambue viajero tembiasa ñepyrũhágui, cultura, ñe'ẽ ha umi tetã iviru sa'ihápe guarã.

en: Although most agencies are willing to take on most regular bookings, many agents specialise in particular types of travel, budget ranges or destinations.
gn: Jepéramo hetave umi agencia ojapyhyséva hetave jejokoyka ojejerurévagui, heta agente ojeheka ambue viaje particular-vape, presupuesto retakué térã moõpa ojehóta.

en: It can be better to use an agent who frequently books similar trips to yours.
gn: Ikatu iporãve ojeiporu peteĩ agente ojapo meméva viaje nde rejaposéva rehegua.

en: Take a look at what trips the agent is promoting, whether on a website or in a shop window.
gn: Ejesareko umi viaje agente oikuave'ẽva rehe, taha'e web renda térã peteĩ escaparate guive.

en: If you want to see the world on the cheap, for necessity, lifestyle or challenge, there are some ways to do that.
gn: Rehechaséramo arapy ehepyme'ẽ heta'ỹme, tekove estilo térã desafío, oĩ ojejapokuaáva ambue hendáicha.

en: Basically, they fall into two categories: Either work while you travel or try and limit your expenses. This article is focused on the latter.
gn: Ñepyrũrã, oñemboja'o mokõi categoría-pe: térã emba'apo ejapo aja viaje térã eñaha'ã emboapýra viru jeiporu. Ko artículo oñemombyte ko ipahávape.

en: For those willing to sacrifice comfort, time and predictability to push expenses down close to zero, see minimum budget travel.
gn: Umi oĩva oheja haguãicha comodidad, aravo ha jehecha tenondeoiporu'ive haguã viru cero ypýpe, eporandu viaje repyrã sa'ivéva rehe.

en: The advice assumes that travellers do not steal, trespass, participate in the illegal market, beg, or otherwise exploit other people for their own gain.
gn: Consejo he'i viajero-kuéra nomondái, ndojapyhýi, ndojoguái mab'eve ilegal, noñakãngetái térã ndoiporúi ambue tapichápe ha'e osẽ porã haguã.

en: An immigration checkpoint is usually the first stop when disembarking from a plane, a ship, or another vehicle.
gn: Peteĩ inmigración ñeñangareko renda ha'e jepi pe jepyta peteĩha ojeguejývo peteĩ avión, peteĩ barco térã ambue mba'yrumýigui.

en: In some cross-border trains inspections are done on the running train and you should have valid ID with you when boarding one of those trains.
gn: Oĩ ambue tren fronterizo, jesareko ojejapohápe tren ryepýpe oku'e aja ha reguereko va'erã identificación oikóva nendive ejupívo ko'ã tren-pe.

en: On night sleeper trains, passports may be collected by the conductor so that you do not have your sleep interrupted.
gn: Umi tren pyareguávape, mboguatahára ombyatykuaa pasaporte-kuéra ani oñemombáy okévape.

en: Registration is an additional requirement for the visa process. In some countries, you must register your presence and address where you are staying with the local authorities.
gn: Registro hína pe jerurepy adicional visa proceso-pe guarã. Ambue tetãme, ejapo va'erã registro reimeha ha tenda repytaha moakãharakuéra upepegua ndive.

en: This might require filling out a form with the local police or a visit to the immigration offices.
gn: Kóva ikatu oikotevẽ oñemyanyhẽ peteĩ formulario policía upepegua ndive térã peteĩ visita inmigración oficina-kuérape.

en: In many countries with such a law, local hotels will handle the registration (make sure to ask).
gn: Heta tetãme peteĩ léi koichagua rehe, hotel-kuéra ojapóta registro rehe (eñeha'ã eporandu)

en: In other cases, only those staying outside of tourist accommodations need to register.  However, this makes the law much more obscure, so find out beforehand.
gn: Ambue jey, umi opytáva alojamiento turístico okaha rehe ojepo va'erã registro. Katu, upéva omopytũeteve léi, péva rehe eporandu va'erã reho mboyve.

en: Architecture is concerned with the design and construction of buildings. The architecture of a place is often a tourist attraction in its own right.
gn: Arquitectura oñangareko ñemoha'ãnga rehe ha ojapo edificio-kuéra rehe. Arquitectura peteĩ tenda pegua py'ỹinte peteĩ atracción turística ijeheguiete.

en: Many buildings are quite beautiful to look at and the view from a tall building or from a cleverly-positioned window can be a beauty to behold.
gn: Heta edificio iporaiterei jahechávo ha ojehecháramo ambue edificio yvatéva guive térã peteĩ ovetã oñemohenda porãva guive ikatu iporãiterei ñande resápe guarã.

en: Architecture overlaps considerably with other fields including urban planning, civil engineering, decorative arts, interior design and landscape design.
gn: Arquitectura ojo'a ambue mba'ére, oike pype táva jehape'apo, ingeniería civil, jeguaka apo, hyapýpe ñembojegua ha paisaje ñemoha'ãnga.

en: Given how remote many of the pueblos are, you won't be able to find a significant amount of nightlife without traveling to Albuquerque or Santa Fe.
gn: Oĩ mombyry rupi ojoehegui tavakuére, nderejuhukuaamo'ãi peteĩ pyharepegua aporã reho'ỹre Albuquerque térã Santa Fe-pe.

en: However, nearly all of the casinos listed above serve drinks, and several of them bring in name-brand entertainment (primarily the large ones immediately surrounding Albuquerque and Santa Fe).
gn: Katu, haimete mayma casino-kuéra oje'e va'ekue oguereko jey'urã, ha heta jey oguereko entretenimiento iporãva (Katuete umi tuichavéva oĩva Albuquerque ha Santa Fe jere rehe.

en: Beware: small-town bars here are not always good places for the out-of-state visitor to hang out.
gn: Ejesarekóke: bar-kuéra oĩva umi táva michĩvévape ndaha'éi katuete tenda iporãva visitante ambue estado-gua ohasa sapy'a haguã.

en: For one thing, northern New Mexico has significant problems with drunk driving, and the concentration of intoxicated drivers is high close to small-town bars.
gn: Peteĩ yképe, Nuevo México norte gotyo oĩ apañuái tuicháva oñemboguata rupi ka'úre mba'yrumýi, ha mboguatahára oka'úva ñembyaty heta umi bar oĩva táva michĩvévape.

en: Unwanted murals or scribble is known as graffiti.
gn: Mural-kuéra térã garabato-kuéra ojeipota'ỹva ojekuaa graffiti-ramo.

en: While it's far from a modern phenomenon, most people probably associate it with youth vandalizing public and private property using spray paint.
gn: Mombyrýramo jepe chugui oiko haguã chugui peteĩ fenómeno ko'agãgua, hetave tapicha hína umi ombojoapýva ko tembiapo umi mitã pyahu ojapóva bandalismo tenda publico ha privado-hápe pintura aerosol-pe.

en: However, nowadays there are established graffiti artists, graffiti events and "legal" walls. Graffiti paintings in this context often resemble artworks rather than illegible tags.
gn: Katu, ko'ágãramo graffiti oñemohenda, graffiti oikóva umi pered "legalva" rehe. Graffiti ñembosa'y ko contexto-pe py'ỹinte ojogua umi mba'ejeguápe umi etiqueta oñeikumby'ỹva rangue.

en: Boomerang throwing is a popular skill that many tourists want to acquire.
gn: Boomerang jepoi hína peteĩ katupyry ojejapoitéva heta turista ojaposéva avei.

en: If you want to learn throwing a boomerang that comes back to your hand, make sure you have a suitable boomerang for returning.
gn: Reikuaaséramo mba'éichapa ojepoi boomerang ou jeýva nde pópe, ehecha va'erã reguerekópa boomerang ou jeykuaáva.

en: Most boomerangs available in Australia are in fact non-returning. It is best for beginners to not try throwing in windy
gn: Hetave umi boomerang oĩva Australia-pe hína umi ndoúi jeýva. Upéva rehe iporãve  ñumi oñepyrũva ani oha'ã oĩ jave yvytu.

en: A Hangi Meal is cooked in a hot pit in the ground.
gn: Peteĩ tembi'u Hangi oñembojy peteĩ pozo akúpe yvýpe.

en: The pit is either heated with hot stones from a fire, or in some places geothermal heat makes areas of ground naturally hot.
gn: Pozo oñembyaku ita akúpe peteĩ ñemyandypeguáva, téraambue tendápe pe geomtríco akukue ojapo umi tenda yvypegua ijeheguíntema haku haguã.

en: The hangi is often used to cook a traditional roast style dinner.
gn: Hangi ojeiporu jepi oñembojy haguã peteĩ cena jepiverõguáicha ñembichy estilo-pe.

en: Several places in Rotorua offer geothermal hangi, while other hangi can be sampled in Christchurch, Wellington and elsewhere.
gn: Heta tenda oĩva Rotorua-pe oikuave'ẽ hangi geotérmico, ha ambue hangi ikatúva ojehechauka Christchurch, Wellington ha ambue tendápe.

en: MetroRail has two classes on commuter trains in and around Cape Town: MetroPlus (also called First Class) and Metro (called Third Class).
gn: Metrorail oguereko mokõichagua tren mohi'aguĩva Cabo távape ha ijerére; MetroPlus (ojeikuaáva avei First Class-ramo) ha Metro (ojeheróva Third Class-ramo)

en: MetroPlus is more comfortable and less crowded but slightly more expensive, though still cheaper than normal metro tickets in Europe.
gn: MetroPlus oguerekove comodidad ha sa'ive tapicha oĩ pype, katu hepymiẽ, katu hepy sa'ive gueteri umi billete ojehepyme'ẽva Europa-pegui.

en: Every train has both MetroPlus and Metro coaches; the MetroPlus coaches are always on the end of the train nearest Cape Town.
gn: Ambue tren-kuéra oguereko vagón-kuéra MetroPlus ha Metro; autocar-kuéra akóinte oĩ tren hiaguĩvéva Cábo tévagui ruguápe.

en: Carrying for others - Never let your bags out of your sight, especially when you are crossing international borders.
gn: Jegueraha ambuévape guarã - Anike araka'eve eipe'a nde maleta nde resa renondégui, ha rejesarekove va'erã rehasajave frontera internacional-va.

en: You could find yourself being used as a drug carrier without your knowledge, which will land you in a great deal of trouble.
gn: Ikatu rejejuhu rejeiporuha oñemohasa haguã droga reikuaa'ỹre, ome'ẽtava ndéve heteiterei apañuái.

en: This includes waiting in line, as drug-sniffing dogs could be used at any time without notice.
gn: Ko'ápe oike fila-pe ñeha'ãrõ, jagua ohetũva ko'ã mba'e ikatu oĩmeraẽ aravópe ojeiporu oñemomarandu'ỹre.

en: Some countries have exceedingly draconian punishments even for first time offenses; these can include prison sentences of over 10 years or death.
gn: Ambue tetã oguereko castigo draconiano-va, jepéramo peteĩ jeýnte oiko ko'ã delito; ko'ãvape oikekuaa carcel-pe jeho 10 ary aja térã jejuka.

en: Unattended bags are a target for theft and can also attract attention from authorities wary of bomb threats.
gn: Voko oñeñangareko'ỹva ikatu oñemonda, ha avei ojesarekokuaa hese mburuvichakuéra oimo'ãkuaágui bomba amenaza-ha.

en: At home, due to this constant exposure to the local germs, odds are very high that you're already immune to them.
gn: Ógape, ñaime rupi expuesto ko'ã germen-kuéra upepeguápe, ikatuete nde repytáma inmune umi mba'épe.

en: But in other parts of the world, where the bacteriological fauna are new to you, you're far more likely to run into problems.
gn: Katu ambue tendápe, bacteria-kuéra fauna ipyahuhápe ndéve guarã, ikatueteve ne apañuái.

en: Also, in warmer climates bacteria both grow more quickly and survive longer outside the body.
gn: Upéicha avei, bacteria-kuéra okakuaa pya'eve ha oikove are tete okaháre.

en: Thus the scourges of Delhi Belly, the Pharaoh's Curse, Montezuma's Revenge, and their many friends.
gn: Upégui ou flegelo Delhi ryepýgui, faraón maldición, Moctezuma ha iñangirũnguéra venganza.

en: As with respiratory problems in colder climates, intestinal problems in hot climates are fairly common and in most cases are distinctly annoying but not really dangerous.
gn: Umi apañuái respiratorio rehegua ro'yhápe, apañuái ñande ryekuépe ára hakuhápe oiko meme jepi ha hetave jey oporoguereko vai katu ndoguerekói peligro.

en: If traveling in a developing country for the first time – or in a new part of the world – don't underestimate the potential culture shock.
gn: Rehóramo peteĩ tetã ojeguerojerávape peteĩha ramo, peteĩ tenda ipyahúvape arapýpe, ani rehechagi peteĩ choque cultural.

en: Many a stable, capable traveler has been overcome by the newness of developing world travel, where many little cultural adjustments can add up quickly.
gn: Heta viajero oho meméva ohasapa kuri chupekuéra mba'e pyahu ohecháva tenda ojeguerojerávape ojapo jave viaje, umi tenda heta ajuste cultural ojejapo va'erãhápe ikatu oñembyaty pya'e.

en: Especially in your initial days, consider splurging on Western-style and -quality hotels, food, and services to help acclimatize.
gn: Katuete oiko umi ára oñepyrũvape, ehecha ikatúpa rejogua hotel-hápe tembi'u ha kuave'ẽmby occidente estilo-pe neipytyvõ haguã eñemohenda haguã rehóvo.

en: Do not sleep on a mattress or pad on the ground in areas where you do not know the local fauna.
gn: Ani rekéti peteĩ colchón térã aramboha ári yvýpe umi tenda reikuaa'ỹhápe mymbakuéra upepegua.

en: If you are going to camp out, bring a camp cot or hammock to keep you away from snakes, scorpions and such.
gn: Ejapóramo comping, eguereu peteĩ tupa'i térã kyha reñemomombyry haguã mbói ha escorpi´pn-kuéragui.

en: Fill your home with a rich coffee in the morning and some relaxing chamomile tea at night.
gn: Emyenyhẽ nde róga peteĩ café hévagui pyharevete ha peteĩ te relajante manzanilla-guigua pyharekue.

en: When you're on a staycation, you have the time to treat yourself and take a few extra minutes to brew up something special.
gn: Reime jave peteĩ estadía-pe, reguereko aravo rejapo haguã rejaposéva ha eguereko haguã ambue aravo'i ejapo haguã peteĩ mba'e especial-va.

en: If you're feeling more adventurous, take the opportunity to juice or blend up some smoothies:
gn: Reñeñandúramo aventuréro-ha, eñepyrũ eñami ha ambojehe'a ambue batido-kuéra:

en: maybe you'll discover a simple beverage that you can make for breakfast when you're back to your daily routine.
gn: Ikatu rejuhu peteĩ jey'urã ndahasýiva ejapokuaáva ñerabosápe guarã ágã reñepyrũ jepyvove nde rekove jepiverõguáicha.

en: If you live in a city with a varied drinking culture, go to bars or pubs in neighborhoods you don't frequent.
gn: Reikóramo peteĩ táva oĩhápe hetaichagua jey'urã, tereho umi bar térã pub oĩva tava'i reho'ỹvape.

en: For those unfamiliar with medical jargon, the words infectious and contagious have distinct meanings.
gn: Ambue tapicha ojepokuaa'ỹva médico-kuéra jerga rehe, ñe'ẽ infeccioso ha contagioso he'ise ambue mba'e.

en: An infectious disease is one that is caused by a pathogen, such as a virus, bacterium, fungus or other parasites.
gn: Peteĩ mba'asy infeccioso hína peteĩ mba'esy omoñepyrũva peteĩ patógeno, ikatu peteĩ virsus, bacteria, hongo térã ambue parásito.

en: A contagious disease is a disease which is easily transmitted by being in the vicinity of an infected person.
gn: Peteĩ ba'asy contagioso hína peteĩ mba'asy oñembohasa pya'e reíva reimehaguére peteĩ tapicha hasýva ypýpe.

en: Many governments require visitors entering, or residents leaving, their countries to be vaccinated for a range of diseases.
gn: Heta tetã rekuáo ojerure umi visitante oike térã osẽva hetãnguéragui omoĩka haguã vacuna hetaichagua mba'asy rehegua.

en: These requirements may often depend on what countries a traveller has visited or intends to visit.
gn: Ko'ã jejerure ikatu oreko dependencia umi tetã viajero ohohaguépe rehe térã ohosehápe.

en: One of strong points of Charlotte, North Carolina, is that it has an abundance of high-quality options for families.
gn: Peteĩ mba'e iporãva Charlotte, Carolina del norte-gua, oguerekoha heta poravorã oikoitéva ogayguakuérape guarã.

en: Residents from other areas often cite family-friendliness as a primary reason for moving there, and visitors often find the city easy to enjoy with children around.
gn: Oikóva ambue tendápe heta jey imandu'a jeguereko porã ogayguaramoguáicha peteĩ mba'e ojehecháva ojeva haguã upépe, ha visitante-kuéra py'ỹinte ojuhu táva ndahasyiveha ojehasa porã haguã mitãnguéra ndive ijerére.

en: In the past 20 years, the amount of child-friendly options in Uptown Charlotte has grown exponentially.
gn: Ko'ã 20 ary ohasávape, poravorã iporãva retakue mitãme guarã Uptow Charlotte-pe okakuaa tuichaiterei.

en: Taxis are not generally used by families in Charlotte, though they can be of some use in certain circumstances.
gn: Taxi-kuéra ndojeiporúi jepi ogayguakuéra rehe Charlotte-pe. Jepéramo ikatu ojeiporu ambue mba'e oikóramo.

en: There is a surcharge for having more than 2 passengers, so this option might be more expensive than necessary.
gn: Oĩ peteĩ recargo ojeguereko jave mokõi pasajero, upéva rehe ko poravoã ikatu hepyve oñeikotevẽvagui.

en: Antarctica is the coldest place on Earth, and surrounds the South Pole.
gn: Antartica ha'e hina pe tenda ho'ysãveva ko yvy ári, ha omongora hina pe Polo Sur.

en: Tourist visits are costly, demand physical fitness, can only take place in summer Nov-Feb, and are largely limited to the Peninsula, Islands and Ross Sea.
gn: Turista kuéra jeju hepy hina, oikoteve estado fisico, ha ikatu ojeju arahaku pente, pea hina Jasypateĩ - Jasykõi jave, ha oje limita hina pe Peninsula renda pe, Isla kuéra ha Yguasu Ross pe.

en: A couple of thousand staff live here in summer in some four dozen bases mostly in those areas; a small number stay over winter.
gn: Peteĩ mokõi su ávakuéra oiko ape arahaku jave irundy docena kuéra basepe, ha hetave umi tendape; pe ara ro'y pe opyta sa'ive.

en: Inland Antarctica is a desolate plateau covered by 2-3 km of ice.
gn: Pe Antartica mbyte ha'e hina peteĩ yvype nandi oguerekova hi'ari 2-3 km yrypy'u.

en: Occasional specialist air tours go inland, for mountaineering or to reach the Pole, which has a large base.
gn: Sapy'ante arapytu especialista kuéra oho imbytepe, ojupy yvyty guasure terã ohupyty pe polo, oimehape peteĩ base tuichava.

en: The South Pole Traverse (or Highway) is a 1600 km trail from McMurdo Station on the Ross Sea to the Pole.
gn: Polo Sur travesía (térã Autopista) hína peteĩ sendero orekóva 1600 km McMurdo estación guive Ross mara-pe Polo peve.

en: It's compacted snow with crevasses filled in and marked by flags. It can only be traveled by specialized tractors, hauling sleds with fuel and supplies.
gn: Upéva hína nieve oñepyrũpyréva ha ojehechauka bandera rupive. Ikatu ojejapo recorrido tractor-kuéra rehe, ogueraháva trineo-kuéra conbustible rehe ha ambue suministro.

en: These aren't very nimble so the trail has to take a long swerve around the Transantarctic Mountains to come onto the plateau.
gn: Ko'ãva ndaipya'éi, upeva rehe sendero oho va'erã peteĩ desvío pukúre montaña transatlántica-kuéra jerére oguahẽ haguã meseta-pe,

en: The most common cause for accidents in winter is slippery roads, pavements (sidewalks) and especially steps.
gn: Upe accidente oikpvéva araro'ýpe hína tape isýĩva, tape yke (acera-kuéra) ha avei escalón-kuéra.

en: At a minimum, you need footwear with suitable soles. Summer shoes are usually very slippery on ice and snow, even some winter boots are deficient.
gn: SA'ivéramo, reikotevẽ peteĩ sapatu orekóva suela tekotevẽva, Sapatu verano-pe guarã isýĩ jepi hielo ha nieve ári, ha upéicha avei oĩ ambue bota araro'ypegua ndoikóiva.

en: The pattern should be deep enough, 5 mm (1/5 inch) or more, and the material soft enough in cold temperatures.
gn: Patrón hypy va'erã, 5mm (1/5 pulgada) térã hetave, ha material oreko va'erã suavidad araro'ýpe guarã.

en: Some boots have studs and there is studded add-on equipment for slippery conditions, suitable for most shoes and boots, for the heels or heels and sole.
gn: Ambue bota oguereko tachuela ha oĩ ambue equipo orekóva clavo tenda isýĩvape guarã, oho hetaichagua sapatu ha bota rehe, tacón-kuéra ha tacón-kuéra suela reheve.

en: Heels should be low and wide. Sand, gravel or salt (calcium chloride) is often scattered on roads or paths to improve traction.
gn: Tacón-kuéra ikarape ha ipe va'erã, yvyku'i, grava térã juky (calcio cloruro) py'ỹinte oñemosarambíva carretera ha tapére omoporãve haguã tracción.

en: Avalanches are not an abnormality; steep slopes can hold only so much slow, and the excess volumes will come down as avalanches.
gn: Avalancha-kuéra oiko meme. Umi pendiente yvate ikatu ojoko sapy'a umi volumen hembýva ha oguejýtava avalancha-ramo.

en: The problem is that snow is sticky, so it needs some triggering to come down, and some snow coming down can be the triggering event for the rest.
gn: Apañuái hína pe nieve oja'imi upéva rehe oikotevẽ activación rehe oguejyve haguã, ha nieve michĩmi nieve ho'va ikatu hína ombojoapykueripa ambue mba'e.

en: Sometimes the original trigging event is the sun warming the snow, sometimes some more snowfall, sometimes other natural events, often a human.
gn: Sapy'ánte, oiko ypýva tingging hína pe kuarahy ombyakúva nieve, sa'ánte ambue nevada-ve, ambue jey evento natural-kuéra, ha py'ỹinte yvyporakuéra.

en: A tornado is a spinning column of very low-pressure air, which sucks the surrounding air inward and upward.
gn: Peteĩ tornado hína peteĩ columna ojeréva orekóva yvytu presión baja rehe, oipytéva pete yvytu oĩva hyepy gotyo ha yvate gotyo.

en: They generate high winds (often 100-200 miles/hour) and can lift heavy objects into the air, carrying them as the tornado moves.
gn: Omboyvytu hatã (sapy'ánte ohupyty jepi 100 guive 200 milla/ hora peve) ha ohupikuaa mba'e pohýi yvate, oguerahávo henondépe ojerévo tornado.

en: They begin as funnels descending from storm clouds, and become "tornadoes" when they touch the ground.
gn: Oñepyrũ embudo oguejývaramo tormaneta araígui, ha oiko chugui tornado opokóvo yvy rehe.

en: Personal VPN (virtual private network) providers are an excellent way of circumventing both political censorship and commercial IP-geofiltering.
gn: VPN ome'ẽ harakuéra tapichápe g̃uarãva (red privada virtual) hína peteĩ mba'e iporãva ojehekýi hag̃ua censura poítica ha IP ñemureko geofiltrado.

en: They are superior to web proxies for several reasons: They re-route all Internet traffic, not only http.
gn: Imbareteve web proxie-kuéragui heta mba'ére: ombohapepaite Internet tráfico, ndaha'éi http año.

en: They normally offer higher bandwidth and better quality of service. They are encrypted and thus harder to spy on.
gn: Jepivéramo oikuave'ẽ jepi peteĩ ancho de banda tuichavéva ha peteĩ kuave'ẽmby iporãvéva. Oreko encriptado ha upéva rehe, hasyve ojeike pype oñema'ẽ haguã.

en: The media companies routinely lie about the purpose of this, claiming that it is to "prevent piracy".
gn: Umi Ñemomaranduha medio ñemuhakuéra ijapu jepi ára ha ára ko mba'épe, he'ívo ojejapoha "ohapejoko haguã piratería".

en: In fact, region codes have absolutely no effect on illegal copying; a bit-for-bit copy of a disk will play just fine on any device where the original will.
gn: Upéicha, umi tendapegua código ndoguerekói mba'éichagua efecto peteĩ copia ilegal ári: peteĩ copia bit ha bit rehe ombohasa jeykuaa oimeraẽ dispositivo ojejapotahápe ype original.

en: The actual purpose is to give those companies more control over their markets; it's all about money spinning.
gn: Upe añetehápe ojeipotáva hína oñeme'ẽ umi ñemuhápe ambue iñemurekokuéra ñesãmbyhy; ha'ekuéra oipota viru ojere ohóvo.

en: Because calls are routed over the Internet, you do not need to use a phone company located where you live or where you travel.
gn: Umi ñehenói ombohape rupi Internet rupive, natekotevẽi ojeiporu peteĩ telefonía ñemuha oĩva reikohápe térã rehohápe.

en: There is also no requirement that you obtain a local number from the community in which you live; you can obtain a satellite Internet connection in the wilds of Chicken, Alaska and select a number which claims you're in sunny Arizona.
gn: Natekotevei avei reguereko peteĩ papapy upe comunidad reikohapeguáva; ikatu reguereko joaju internet-pe petei satélite rupive Chicken naturaleza-pe ha eiporavo haguã peteĩ papapy oĩha Arizona-pe, tenda ikuarahy memévape.

en: Often, you have to buy a global number separately that allows PSTN phones to call you. Where the number is from makes a difference for people calling you.
gn: Py'ỹinte, ejogua va'erã peteõ papapy global ijyképe ohejáva umi pumbyry RTC nderenói. Moõgui ou upe papapy omoambue umi tapicha nerenóivape guarã.

en: Real-time text translator apps – applications that are capable of automatically translating whole segments of text from one language into another.
gn: Jehaipy ñe’ẽasa rehegua aplicación ko’ag̃aitegua: ha’e peteĩ aplicación ikatúva ojapo ñe’ẽasa ijeguieténte jehaipy oĩhaguéicha peteĩ ñe’ẽgui ambue ñe’ẽme.

en: Some of the applications in this category can even translate texts in foreign languages on signs or other objects in the real world when the user points the smartphone towards those objects.
gn: Oĩ aplicación ko categoría rehegua ikatúva ojapo jehaipy ñe’ẽasa ambue tetãmegua ñe’ẽme cartel térã ambue mba’épe yvy’ape’árigua oikoteéva puruhára omoĩramo pumbyry upe mba’e rovaque gotyo.

en: The translation engines have improved dramatically, and now often give more or less correct translations (and more seldom gibberish), but some care is due, as they still may have gotten it all wrong.
gn: Ñe’ẽasa ñemboguatarã oĩva tuicha oñemomorãve, ha ko’ag̃a py’ỹínte aimetéma osẽ porãmba ñe’ẽasa (ha sapy’ánte noñeikumbýi), hakatu oñeñangarekova’erã hese, ikatu rupi oĩgueteri jejavy.

en: One of the most prominent apps in this category is Google Translate, which allows offline translation after downloading the desired language data.
gn: Peteĩ aplicación ojehecharamovéva hína ko categoríape ha’e Google Translate, ohejáva ojejapo ñe’ẽasa conexión- ỹrehe oñemboguejy rire dato- kuéra ñe’ẽ ojeipotávagui.

en: Using GPS navigation apps on your smartphone can be the easiest and most convenient way to navigate when out of your home country.
gn: GPS aplicación navegación rehegua jeporu ne pumbyry ha’evévape ikatu hína ndahasyive ha oikoiteve navegación-rã eimévove okápe ne retãgui.

en: It can save money over buying new maps for a GPS, or a standalone GPS device or renting one from a car rental company.
gn: Ikatu hembyve ndéve viru ejoguakuévo mapa-kuéra GPS-peguarã térã peteĩ dispositivo GPS ha’eñokuaáva terã eipuru peteĩ, peteĩ empresa ojepurukahápe mba’yrumỹ’i.

en: If you don't have a data connection for your phone, or when it is out of range, their performance can be limited or unavailable.
gn: Ndereguerekóiramo conexión dato-kuéra rehegua nde pumbyrymỹipe g̃uarã, térã ndohupytýirama, rendimiento oguerekóva ikatu sa’ive térã ndoguerekoi’etevoi.

en: Every corner store is filled with a confusing array of pre-paid phone cards that can be used from pay phones or ordinary telephones.
gn: Opaite ñemuhã oĩva opárupi henyhẽ ipype opaichagua pumbyry tarjeta jehepyme’ẽmbyréma ikatúva ojeporu opavavépe g̃uarã pumbyry ha pumbyry común-peve."

en: While most cards are good for calling anywhere, some specialise in providing favourable call rates to specific groups of countries.
gn: Jepéramo hetave oĩ tarjeta oikóva oñehenói hag̃ua oimehápe, oĩ katu ojeporúva ome’ẽvo tarifa ñehenóirã umi aty oñemoneĩmbyréva tetãnguéra hárupi.

en: Access to these services is often through a toll-free telephone number that can be called from most phones without charge.
gn: Ojeike hag̃ua ko’ã ñekuave’ẽmbýpe hetajey ojejapo peteĩ pumbyry papapy ojehepyme’ẽ’ỹva guive ikatuhápe oñehenói kuaahápe jehepyme’ẽ’ỹ rehe oimeháichagua pumbyrýgui.

en: Rules regarding regular photography also apply to video recording, possibly even more so.
gn: Fotografía ñeguenohẽme g̃uarã mbojojahakuéra ha’e avei ojeporúva video aporã ojeporúva, ha ikatu hetave avei.

en: If just taking a photo of something isn't allowed, then you shouldn't even think of recording a video of it.
gn: Ojehapejokóramo foto ñeguenohẽ peteĩ mba’égui, upéicharamo ni gua’uhápe ndaikatumo’ãi ojapo peteĩ video upe mba’égui.

en: If using a drone, check well in advance as to what you are allowed to film and what permits or additional licensing are required.
gn: Eipurúramo peteĩ dron, ehecharaẽva’erã mba’épa ikatu egrava ha mba’éichagua kuatia ñemoneĩpa eikotevẽ.

en: Flying a drone near an airport or over a crowd is almost always a bad idea, even if it's not illegal in your area.
gn: Dron ñemboveve peteĩ aeropuerto ykérehe térã yvypóra aty ári na’ipórãi ojejapo, ni ndaha’éiramo ilegal eimehápe.

en: Nowadays air travel is only rarely booked directly through the airline without first searching and comparing prices.
gn: Ko’agãro g̃uarã, umi viaje aéreo-kuéra sa’íma ojejogua aerolínea renda rupive ojeheka’ỹre ha oñembohovake mboyve mboýpa hepykue.

en: Sometimes the same flight can have vastly differing prices at various agregators and it pays to compare search results and to also look at the website of the airline itself before booking.
gn: Sapy’ánte, upe jeroveve voi oguerekokuaa jhepyme’ẽ ojoavýva umimba’e oñemoĩvévape ha iporã ñambohovake umi jehekápe ojejuhúva ha avei oñema’ẽva’erã aerolínea web renda rehe ojejogua mboyve.

en: While you may not need a visa for short visits to certain countries as a tourist or for business, going there as an international student generally requires a longer stay than going there just as a casual tourist.
gn: Ikatúramo jepe noñekotevẽi peteĩ visa jehosapy’arã ambue tetãme, turista térã ñemba’aporã, avei ñemoaranduverã pyỹínte oikotevẽ jepytave umi turista sapy’aguánte.

en: In general, staying in any foreign country for an extended period of time will require you to obtain a visa in advance.
gn: Hetave jey, jepyta areve peteĩ tetã ambuépe oñeikotevẽta peteĩ visa jehomboyveguáva.

en: Student visas generally have different requirements and application procedures from normal tourist or business visas.
gn: Temimbo’e ñemoaranduverã visa py’ỹínte oguereko requisito ha procedimiento jejerure rehegua iñambuéva umi visa-kuéra  ojeporúva turismo térã ñemba’aporãgui.

en: For most countries, you will need an offer letter from the institution you wish to study at, and also evidence of funds to support yourself for at least the first year of your course.
gn: Hetave tetãnguérape , oñeikotevẽta peteĩ carta oferta upe mbo’ehaovusu oñemoarandutahágui ha avei ohechaukava’erã viru oikove hag̃ua jepevéramo ary peteĩha paha peve.

en: Check with the institution, as well as the immigration department for the country you wish to study in for detailed requirements.
gn: Ehecháke mbo’ehaovusúpe, upéicha avei inmigración renda omotenondéva upe tetã eñemoaradusehápe, eikuaa hag̃ua mba’e mba’épa ojejerure.

en: Unless you are a diplomat, working overseas generally means that you will have to file income tax in the country you are based in.
gn: Ndaha’éiramo diplomático mba’e, tetã ambuépe ñemba’apo he’ise hína ombokuatiaa ha ohepyme’ẽva’erã renta impuesto rehegua tetã oihápegua.

en: Income tax is structured differently in different countries, and the tax rates and brackets vary widely from one country to another.
gn: Renta rehegua impuesto oñemohenda ha oñemoambue tetãnguérape, ha umi tasa impositiva  ha tramo-kuéra oñemoambue avei tetãnguéra apytépe.

en: In some federal countries, such as the United States and Canada, income tax is levied both at the federal level and at the local level, so the rates and brackets can vary from region to region.
gn: Oĩ umi tetãnguéra federal-va, taha’e Estados Unidos ha Canadá, renta rehegua impuesto ojehepyme’ẽ nivel federal ha avei nivel local-pe, upévare tasa ha tramo-kuéra ikatu iñambue ojuehegui umi tavaháre.

en: While immigration check is usually absent or a formality when you arrive in your homeland, customs control can be a hassle.
gn: Jepéramo inmigración-pegua control ndaipóri guasúi térã ha’e peteĩ formalidad eg̃uahẽvo ne retãme, hakatu aduana-pegua jejesareko ikatu ñanemyangekõi.

en: Make sure you know what you can and cannot bring in and declare anything over the legal limits.
gn: Ehechaporãke mba’épa eguerukuaa ha mb’aépa ndaikatúi ha emombe’upaite opamba’e mbojojahakuéra ári oĩva.

en: The easiest way to get started in the business of travel writing is to hone your skills on an established travel blog website.
gn: Viaje jehaipy negocio-pe jeike ndahasýive, eñemoarandúvo blogs web renda oĩmavavoi jeporukuaápe.

en: After you become comfortable with formatting and editing on the web, then later, you might create your own website.
gn: Eñeñanduporãmavo ko formato-pe ha web ñemoambuépe, ikaku emoñepyrũ web renda nemba’eteéva.

en: Volunteering while travelling is a great way to make a difference but it's not just about giving.
gn: Eikóramo voluntario-ramo umi viaje ejapo aja, pea nemoambuéta tapichakuéragui, hakatu ndaha’éi eme’ẽnteva’erã avei.

en: Living and volunteering in a foreign country is a great way to get to know a different culture, meet new people, learn about yourself, get a sense of perspective and even gain new skills.
gn: Jeiko ha voluntario avei tetã ambuépe ha peteĩ tape pora eikuaauvévo arandupy nemba’e’ỹva, eikuaa yvypóra pyehu, ejeikuaave nde voi, ema’ẽ ambueháicha ha avei eguereko katupyry pyahu.

en: It can also be a good way to stretch a budget to allow a longer stay somewhere since many volunteer jobs provide room and board and a few pay a small salary.
gn: Avei ikatu voluntario rupi ikatu viru eguerekóva areve reipúru umi eikohárupi, ume voluntario rembiapo rehe ikatu oñeme’ẽ ndéve reiko hag̃uã ha ojehepyme’ẽ ndéve michĩmi.

en: Vikings used the Russian waterways to get to the Black Sea and Caspian Sea. Parts of these routes can still be used. Check possible need for special permits, which may be difficult to get.
gn: Umi vikingo-kuéra oipuruva’ekueRusia-pegua ygarata rape og̃uahẽ hag̃ua Mar Negro ha Mar Caspio-pe. Ikatu gueteri ojeporu ko’ã tape. Ehechajeýke eikotevẽtaramo permiso, ikatúva hasy hína ijeguereko.

en: The White Sea–Baltic Canal connects the Arctic Ocean to the Baltic Sea, via Lake Onega, Lake Ladoga and Saint Petersburg, mostly by rivers and lakes.
gn: Mar Blanco . Báltico Canal ombojoaju Océano Ártico Mar Báltico rehe, lago Onega rupive, lago Ladoga ha San Petersburgo, jetavejey ysyry guasu ha yno’õ rupive.

en: Lake Onega is also connected to Volga, so coming from the Caspian Sea through Russia is still possible.
gn: Lago Onega avei ojoaju Volga rehe, upéva rupi ikatugueteri oñeg̃uahẽ Mar Caspio guive Rusia rupive.

en: Be assured that once you hit the marinas everything will be pretty obvious. You will meet other boat hitchhikers and they will share their information with you.
gn: Eikuaaporãke eg̃uahẽvo peteĩ puerto deportivo-hápe, oĩvante oĩporãta. Upépe eikuaáta ambue autostopistas ygaratepegua ha ha’ekuéra omombe’úta ndéve oikuaáva guive.

en: Basically you'll be putting up notices offering your help, pacing the docks, approaching people cleaning their yachts, trying to make contact with sailors in the bar, etc.
gn: Sa’ivéramo ejejuhúta emoĩhápe marandu eikuave’ẽhápe neñeipytyvõ , eikundaha umi muelle-hárupi, eñemoag̃uívo umi tapicha omopotĩva yjygarata, eñomongetase katui umi marinero-kuéra ndive bar-hárupi.

en: Try to talk to as many people as possible. After a while everyone will know you and will give you hints as to which boat is looking for someone.
gn: Eñeha’ã eñomongeta tapichakuéra ndive hetave ikatuháicha. Upe riréminte, opavave nde kuaáta ha oñembe’úta ndéve mba’e ygaratápa oheka hína mba’apohára.

en: You should choose your Frequent Flyer airline in an alliance carefully.
gn: Eiporavo porãva’erã nde aerolínea evevejepiha peteĩ alianza-pe.

en: Although you may think it is intuitive to join the airline you fly most, you should be aware that privileges offered are often different and frequent flyer points may be more generous under a different airline in the same alliance.
gn: Jepéramo ikatu eñakangeta ha’eha intuitívo ejoaju upe aerolínea katui evevéjepiha, ehechakuaava’erã umi privilegio oñeikuave’ẽva py’ÿinte iñambue ha umi oikundaháva ohovejepiha ikatu oguereko hetave mba’e peteĩ aerolínea iñambuéva  upe alianza jeseguáva.

en: Airlines such as Emirates, Etihad Airways, Qatar Airways & Turkish Airlines have greatly expanded their services to Africa, and offer connections to many major African cities at competitive rates than other European airlines.
gn: Umi aerolínea-kuéra ha’eháicha Emirates, Etihad Airways, Qatar Airways ha Turkish Airlines tuicha omongakuaa iñekuave’ẽ Africa-pe ha oikuave’ẽ hikuái umi tavusu ha’evévape africa-peguápe, sa’ive viru rehe ambue aerolínea europea-gui.

en: Turkish Airlines flies to 39 destinations in 30 African countries as of 2014.
gn: Turkish Airlines oveve 39 tendápe 30 tetãme Africa-ryepýpe 2014-guive.

en: If you have additional travel time, check to see how your total fare quote to Africa compares with a round-the-world fare.
gn: Eguerekóramo tiempo ñeikundaha adicional-rã, ejesareko mba’éichapa oñombohovake tarifa repy Africa-pe peteĩ tarifa yvy ape ári jerére ndive.

en: Don't forget to add in the extra costs of additional visas, departure taxes, ground transportation, etc. for all those places outside of Africa.
gn: Ani nderesarái emoĩve hag̃ua umi costo adicional, ñeikundaha adicional rehegua, ñesẽ rehegua impuesto, mba’yrumÿi ha hetave mba’e; oparupitepe g̃uarã umi tenda okaháre Africa-gui.

en: If you want to fly around the world completely in the Southern Hemisphere, the choice of flights and destinations is limited due to the lack of transoceanic routes.
gn: Ejereséramo yvy’ape jerére tuichakue javeve hemisferio sur-pe, upe jeveve jeiporavo ha tenda ojehoha oguereko límite ndaipóri rupi tapekuéra transoceánica.

en: No airline alliance covers all three ocean crossings in the Southern Hemisphere (and SkyTeam covers none of the crossings).
gn: Ndaipóri aerolínea alianza og̃uahẽva mbohapyve cruce oceánico hemisferio sur-pe ( ha SkyTeam  nog̃uahẽi ni peteĩ umi cruce-pe

en: However, Star Alliance covers everything except the eastern South Pacific from Santiago de Chile to Tahiti, which is a LATAM Oneworld flight.
gn: Hakatu, Star Aliance aimete og̃uahẽmba, nog̃uahẽi Santiago de Chile este gotyo, Pacífico Sur-pe Tahiti-peve, ha’éva LATAM Oneworld rape.

en: This flight is not the only option if you want to skip the South Pacific and the west coast of South America. (see below)
gn: Ko jeroveve ndaha’éi opción año oĩva nderehoséiramo Pacífico Sur ha América del Sur Costa oeste-pe (ehecha yvýpe)

en: In 1994, the ethnically Armenian Nagorno-Karabakh region of Azerbaijan waged war against the Azeris.
gn: 1994-pe, Nagorno-Karabaj  Azerbaiyán tavusupegua, etnia armenia, oñorãirõ umi azeríes ndive.

en: With Armenian backing, a new republic was created. However, no established nation - not even Armenia - officially recognizes it.
gn: Armenia ñepytyvõme, oñemoheñói peteĩ república pyahu. Hakatu, nipeteĩ tetã oñemopyendámava, ni Armenia, nomoneĩri gueteri.

en: Diplomatic arguments over the region continue to mar relations between Armenia and Azerbaijan.
gn: Umi diplomático-kuéra ñembojovake tavusu rehegua apañuĩ, ohapejoko gueteri ñemyesakã  Armenia y Azerbaiyán ojoapytépe.

en: The Canal District (Dutch: Grachtengordel) is the famous 17th-century district surrounding the Binnenstad of Amsterdam.
gn: Upe Canal Distrito ( holandés-pe: Grachtengordel) ha’e  ojekuaáitemíva tavusu siglo XVII ojeréva Ámsterdam Binnenstad rehe.

en: The whole district is designated as a UNESCO World Heritage Site for its unique cultural and historical value, and its property values are among the highest of the country.
gn: Upe tavusu ojehechakuaa Patrimonio de la Humanidad-ramo, UNESCOrehe hembiasa porãmby ha arandupy ijojaha’ỹva, ha yvyvy vore hepykue oĩ ijyvatevéva apytépe tetã ryepýpe.

en: Cinque Terre, which means Five Lands, comprises the five small coastal villages of Riomaggiore, Manarola, Corniglia, Vernazza and Monterosso located in the Italian region of Liguria.
gn: Cinque Terre, he’iséva Po Yvyvore, oĩ ipype umi po tava’i hembe’yrehua Riomaggiore,  Manarola, Corniglia, Vernazza y Monterosso oĩva Italia-voreguasúpe hérava Liguria.

en: They are listed on the UNESCO World Heritage List.
gn: Oĩ hikuái Patrimonio Mundial UNESCO-pegua rysýipe.

en: Over the centuries, people have carefully built terraces on the rugged, steep landscape right up to the cliffs that overlook the sea.
gn: Heta siglo ryepýpe, yvypóra omopu’ã mbeguekatuete terraza-kuéra, paisaje upe yvytyty mbytépe ha ysyry guasu rembe’ýpe.

en: Part of its charm is the lack of visible corporate development. Paths, trains and boats connect the villages, and cars cannot reach them from the outside.
gn: Ojehecharamovéva upépe upe sa’i ñeakãrapu’ã ojekuaáva. Tapekuéra, mba’yrumỹpuku ha ygarata ombojoaju ojuehe umi tava’i ha mba’yrumỹi ndaikatúi og̃uahẽ upépe okahaguio.

en: The varieties of French which are spoken in Belgium and Switzerland differ slightly from the French spoken in France, though they are similar enough to be mutually intelligible.
gn: Francia ñe’ẽ variedad oñeñe’ẽva Bélgica ha Suiza-pe sa’i ojoavy ñe’ẽ francés oñe’ñe’ẽva Francia-pe, jepéramo ojojogua mokõive sa’i oñoikumby.

en: In particular, the numbering system in French-speaking Belgium and Switzerland has some slight peculiarities that are different from the French spoken in France, and the pronunciation of some words is slightly different.
gn: Upévare, Bélgica ha Suiza jepapapy ñemohenda oñe’ẽva francés, oguereko sa’imi imba’e teéva ndojokupytýiva francés ñe’ẽ oñeñe’ẽva Francia-pe, ha ambue ñe’ẽ he’iséva iñambue’imi ojuhegui.

en: Nevertheless, all French-speaking Belgians and Swiss would have learned standard French in school, so they would be able to understand you even if you used the standard French numbering system.
gn: Jepéramo, opavave belga ha suizo-kuéra francófonos , oñemoaranduva’ekue francés stándar-pe mbo’ehaópe, upévagui ikatu oikumby eréva jepéramo eipurúramo francés jeipapapy ypýva.

en: In many parts of the world, waving is a friendly gesture, indicating "hello."
gn: Heta hendápe yvy’ape’ári, ñemomaitei peteĩ gesto imarangatúva he’iséva “maitei”.

en: However, in Malaysia, at least among the Malays in rural areas, it means "come over," similar to the index finger bent toward the body, a gesture which is used in certain Western countries, and should be used only for that purpose.
gn: Jepéramo, Malasia-pe, umi malayo okaháregua ojoapytépe, he’ise “eju”, ha’ete ku kua’ã índice ojepokáva rete gotyo, peteĩ gesto ojeporúva ambue tetã occidental-pe ha ojeporu pearãiténte.

en: Similarly, a British traveller in Spain may mistake a wave goodbye involving the palm facing the waver (rather than the person being waved at) as a gesture to come back.
gn: Upéicha avei, peteĩ viajero británico España-pe ikatu ojavy peteĩ despedida oipuruhápe ipopyte tapicha omomaiteívape ( ndaha’éi tapicha oñemomaitéivape) peteĩ gesto ojeju hag̃ua.

en: Auxiliary languages are artificial or constructed languages created with the intent of facilitating communication between peoples who would otherwise have difficulty communicating.
gn: Umi ñe’ẽnguéra jeykerorãva ha ñe’ẽ  gua’únte térã ojejepóva tavusu ha tekoha oñoikumby porãve hag̃ua, upeva’ỹre hasyvéta oñoikumby porã hag̃ua ojoapytépe.

en: They are separate from lingua francas, which are natural or organic languages that become dominant for one reason or another as means of communication between speakers of other languages.
gn: Ojei ojuehegui umi lingua francas-gui, ha’éva ñe’ẽ  rapo orgánica-va oikova ichugui dominante taha’e ha’eva razón-rehe oñoikumby hag̃ua ojoapytépe ambue ñe’ẽ nguéra ndive.

en: In the heat of the day, travelers can experience mirages that give the illusion of water (or other things).
gn: Árahakukuépe, umi viajero-kuéra ikatu ohasa espejismo ome’ẽ va e ilusión (ha ambue mba’e)

en: These can be dangerous if the traveler pursue the mirage, wasting precious energy and remaining water.
gn: Ko’ã ikatu ivai umi visitante-pe guarã ohapykuhóramo upe espejismo, oipuru rei imbaretekue ha y oguerekóva rembyre.

en: Even the hottest of deserts can become extremely cold at night. Hypothermia is a real risk without warm clothing.
gn: Avei umi desierto hakuvéva ikatu pyharekue ro’yeterei. Hipotermia ha’e peteĩ riesgo avei ao haku’ỹ rehe.

en: In summer, especially, you'll need to watch out for mosquitoes if you decide to hike through the rainforest.
gn: Arahakúpe jave, eñeñangarekova’erã ñati’ũgui esẽ esẽ ramo eguata selva tropical rehe.

en: Even if you're driving through the subtropical rainforest, a few seconds with the doors open while you get inside the vehicle is enough time for mosquitoes to get in the vehicle with you.
gn: Upéicha avei ehojave mba’yrumỹime selva tropical subtropical rehe, nerokẽ  nerembotýiramo sapya’aitépe oikepaitéta ñati’ũ.

en: Bird flu, or more formally avian influenza, can infect both birds and mammals.
gn: Gripe aviar, térã influenza aviar, ikatu ombohasy mymba ovevéva ha avei mymba okambúvape.

en: Fewer than a thousand cases have ever been reported in humans, but some of them have been fatal.
gn: Oñemombe’u oĩha sa’ive mil caso-gui yvypóra apytépe, hakatu umíva apytépe oĩ oprojukáva.

en: Most have involved people who work with poultry, but there is also some risk to birdwatchers.
gn: Hetave hasýva oĩ yvypóra omba’apóva corral-pegua mymba ovevevakuéra ndive, ha avei oĩ riesgo umi  corral-pegua mymba observador.

en: Typical for Norway are steep fjords and valleys that suddenly give way to a high, more or less even plateau.
gn: Noruega-peguaite hina umi fiordos escarpados ha valle-kuéra, osẽva sapy’a umi meseta yvate, vai vai ojojáva.

en: These plateaus are often referred to as "vidde" meaning a wide, open treeless space, a boundless expanse.
gn: Ko’ã meseta-kuéra py’ỹínte ojehero “vidde”, he’iséva tenda guasu inandíva yvyramáta ỹrehe, ha ijapyra’ỹva.

en: In Rogaland and Agder they are usually called "hei" meaning a treeless moorland often covered in heather.
gn: Rogaland ha Agder-pe ojehero jepi “hei”, he’iséva peteĩ páramo yvyraraka’ỹrehe ha py’ỹínte ojako’i upépe brezo-kuéra."

en: The glaciers are not stable, but flow down the mountain. This will cause cracks, crevasses, which may be obscured by snow bridges.
gn: Umi glaciar-kuéra ndaha’éi estable, hakatu osẽ yvytyty yvyguivo. Kóva ombohapéta grieta, ikuaha ikatúva opyta kañyhamẽ umi puente de nieves rehe.

en: The walls and roofs of ice caves can collapse and cracks can get closed.
gn: Umi ogyke ha ogahoja yrypy’a cueva rehegua ikatu ho’a ha umi grieta ikatu oñemboty.

en: At the edge of glaciers huge blocks break loose, fall down and perhaps jump or roll farther from the edge.
gn: Umi glaciar-kuéra rembe’ýre, tuicha tuicháva vore ojei, ho’a ha ikatu opo térã osysryry mombyryve hembe’ýgui.

en: The tourist season for the hill stations generally peaks during the Indian summer.
gn: Temporada turística umi yvytyty estación-kuérapeg̃uarã, hetave jey ohupyty heko ha’evéva indio arahaku’aja.

en: However, they have a different kind of beauty and charm during winter, with many hill stations receiving healthy amounts of snow and offering activities such as skiing and snowboarding.
gn: Hakatu, oguereko iporãmby ambuéva ha ojehecharamóva araro’y aja, oĩ heta yvytyty rehegua estación-kuéra og̃uahẽhápe nieve hesãiva hetaiterei ha oikuaveẽ jejaporã esquí ha snowboard.

en: Only a few airlines still offer bereavement fares, which slightly discount the cost of last-minute funeral travel.
gn: Sa’i oĩ umi aerolínea oikuave’ẽva ñemano rehegua tarifa, ombosa’ivéva michĩmi umi viaje funerario jehepyme’ẽ aravo pahápema ojejeruréva.

en: Airlines that offer these include Air Canada, Delta Air Lines, Lufthansa for flights originating from the U.S. or Canada, and WestJet.
gn: Umi oikuave’ẽva ko’ãmba’e ha’e Air Canada, Delta Air Lines, Lufthansa umi jeveve osẽva EE. UU. Térã Canadá ha WestJet.

en: In all cases, you must book by phone directly with the airline.
gn: Opaháichante, ojehechámava’erã reserva pumbyry rupive aerolínea ndive.
